data.title: Gas utility UGI fiscal 2025 adjusted EPS beats expectations, helped by utilities segment
data.description:
Overview

  • UGI fiscal 2025 adjusted EPS beats analyst expectations

  • UGI fiscal 2025 revenue missed analyst expectations

Outlook

  • UGI projects fiscal 2026 adjusted EPS between $2.90 and $3.15

  • UGI extends EPS CAGR to 5-7% between FY24 and FY29

Result Drivers

  • AMERIGAS IMPROVEMENTS - Higher EBIT at AmeriGas contributed to strong fiscal 2025 results, according to CEO Robert Flexon

  • UTILITIES PERFORMANCE - Solid operational performance from the Utilities segment supported fiscal 2025 results

  • TAX BENEFITS - Significant tax benefits were a key driver of UGI's exceptional fiscal 2025 results

Key Details

Metric

Beat/Miss

Actual

Consensus Estimate

FY Revenue

Miss

$7.29 bln

$7.54 bln (2 Analysts)

FY Adjusted EPS

Beat

$3.32

$3.18 (3 Analysts)

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "strong buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy", no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the natural gas utilities peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for UGI Corp is $41.00, about 14.3% above its November 20 closing price of $35.13

  • The stock recently traded at 11 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 11 three months ago

Press Release:

data.title: Worley Price Target Cut 1.6% to A$15.75/Share by Macquarie
Blockchain Venture Capital Inc. Welcomes Draft of Bank of Canada's Stablecoin Act
data.description:
Toronto, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - November 20, 2025) - Blockchain Venture Capital Inc. ("BVCI" or the "Company"), the issuer of the Canadian-dollar-backed stablecoin CADT and U.S.-dollar-backed CUSD, welcomes the release of the Draft of the Bank of Canada's Stablecoin Act, representing a landmark step toward establishing a clear, nationally supervised regulatory regime for fiat-backed stablecoins in Canada.

The proposed legislation sets out comprehensive requirements to enhance consumer protection, financial stability, and operational integrity. Key elements of the Draft Act include:

  • A clear ban on yield or interest associated with fiat-backed stablecoin deposits;
  • Mandatory, publicly available redemption policies for stablecoin holders;
  • Strict reserve rules, requiring that reserve assets:
    • Be used solely for redemptions;
    • Be composed of the reference currency or high-quality liquid assets (HQLA);
    • Be held with a qualified custodian, defined as a Canadian financial institution;
  • Confirmation that compliant fiat-backed stablecoins will not be treated as securities;
  • Enhanced national-security, operational reliability, and risk-management obligations;
  • A new authorization and oversight framework administered by the Bank of Canada.

These requirements reflect the direction set out in Budget 2025, affirming the federal government's commitment to a safe, transparent, and domestically regulated stablecoin environment.

BVCI Reaffirms Its Commitment to Meeting All Requirements

BVCI has consistently designed CADT and CUSD around transparency, qualified custody, 1:1 reserve backing, and strong compliance. Over the past five years, the Company has worked with Canadian regulators and banking partners to uphold robust governance, AML/ATF standards, reserve safeguards, and consumer-protection measures.

"BVCI welcomes the clarity and direction introduced in the Draft Stablecoin Act," said Richard Zhou, Founder and CEO of BVCI. "From day one, CADT and CUSD have been built around the principles now being formalized by the Bank of Canada. We remain fully committed to meeting all criteria set out in the Draft Act, including the ban on yield, strict reserve rules, transparent redemption practices, and adherence to qualified custodian requirements."

Once the Act is approved by Parliament, BVCI intends to participate in the Bank of Canada's authorization regime and operate its stablecoin systems under full national oversight.

BVCI's Early Leadership in Stablecoin Development

BVCI has been a pioneer in Canadian and international stablecoin development since 2019:

  • July 1, 2019 - Launched the CADT stablecoin in Toronto, helping "launch Canada into a new era of digital global finance."
  • November 2019 - Introduced the CUSD stablecoin, pegged to the U.S. dollar, in Nevada, U.S.
  • July 2020 - Issued CADT in Canada with the support of Concentra Trust, becoming one of the earliest trust-backed stablecoins linked to the Canadian dollar.
  • 2019-2025 - Worked closely with Ontario, Alberta, and British Columbia securities regulators under CSA oversight, advocating for clear, practical rules for stablecoin issuance, security-tokenization, and Exempt Market Dealer (EMD) registration.
  • August 12, 2022 - Listed on the Canadian Securities Exchange , becoming one of the first Canadian public companies with active blockchain infrastructure, stablecoin products, and payment solutions.
  • December 2023-2025 - Submitted Exemptive Relief Applications in accordance with CSA Staff Notice 21-333, seeking approval to enable CADT trading on major Crypto Trading Platforms (CTPs).
  • However, given the emergence of the new federal stablecoin regulatory regime, BVCI may consider either continuing or withdrawing these applications depending on the final definition and scope of 'value-referenced crypto assets' and 'stablecoin issuers' under the Bank of Canada's forthcoming Stablecoin Act.

Despite years of regulatory uncertainty, BVCI has remained steadfast in its mission to build secure, compliant, and consumer-friendly digital-currency systems that bridge traditional finance and blockchain technology.

Strengthening Canada's Digital Asset and Payment Landscape

The introduction of the Draft Stablecoin Act marks a transformational moment for Canada's digital finance sector. As stablecoins increasingly support payments, settlements, commerce, and blockchain-based services, a nationally supervised framework is essential for protecting consumers and fostering innovation.

"This framework represents a major step forward for compliant digital payments in Canada," added Zhou. "We look forward to contributing our experience and technology to support the successful rollout of this national regime."

The Draft Act is available at:

https://fin.canada.ca/drleg-apl/2025/nwmm-amvm-1-1125-bil.pdf

Termination of Acquisition of Coin Shack

After constructive discussions and mutual agreement, BVCI and Coin Shack determined not to proceed with the previously announced acquisition. This decision was previously disclosed in BVCI's earlier news release and reflects both parties' shared assessment of regulatory considerations and strategic priorities.

About Blockchain Venture Capital Inc.

Blockchain Venture Capital Inc. is an Ontario-incorporated company registered as a Money Services Business (MSB) with FINTRAC. The Company provides blockchain-based financial infrastructure, including:

  • BVC Chain, a proprietary blockchain and distributed-ledger platform;
  • BvcPay, a mobile wallet enabling digital transactions using Bitcoin, Ethereum, and CADT;
  • CADT, BVCI's Canadian-dollar-backed stablecoin for payments, settlements, digital-asset issuance, and on-chain ledger services.

Unless and until BVCI obtains all necessary regulatory approvals or qualifies for exemptions, there is no assurance the Company will be able to proceed with its CADT-related initiatives.

For Further Information, Please Contact:

Richard Zhou

Chairman & CEO

Blockchain Venture Capital Inc.

Email: service@bvcadt.com

Forward-Looking Information

This news release contains forward-looking information within the meaning of applicable securities laws, including expectations regarding acquisitions, private placements, and regulatory matters. Although the Company has attempted to identify important factors that may cause actual results to differ materially, other factors may cause results not to be as anticipated, estimated, or intended. There can be no assurance that such information will prove to be accurate. Readers should not place undue reliance on forward-looking information. The Company does not undertake to update forward-looking information except in accordance with applicable securities laws.

The Canadian Securities Exchange has not reviewed and does not accept responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

Not for distribution to U.S. newswire services or dissemination in the United States.

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/275400

data.title: Joby Aviation sues air-taxi rival Archer over trade secrets
data.description:
By Blake Brittain

Electric air-taxi company Joby Aviation has sued rival Archer Aviation in California state court for allegedly stealing its trade secrets, according to a complaint made public on Thursday.

Joby said in the lawsuit, filed on Wednesday in state court in Santa Cruz, California, that Archer hired away a Joby employee who took confidential information to Archer about its business strategies, partnership terms and aircraft specifications.

The employee, George Kivork, and spokespeople for Archer did not immediately respond to requests for comment. A Joby spokesperson declined to comment beyond the text of the complaint.

Electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft firms like Joby and Archer are racing to bring their vehicles to market, aiming to meet a demand for faster, more sustainable urban transportation.

Santa Cruz, California-based Joby is backed by Toyota and said in September that it plans to bring helicopter and seaplane services to Uber's ride-sharing app as soon as next year.

Joby's lawsuit said Kivork, who led its state and local policy team, left the company for San Jose, California-based Archer after his last working day in July.

Joby alleged that Archer misused its trade secrets in an August bid to undercut Joby's contract with a real-estate developer. The developer told Joby that Archer knew confidential details of the agreement and that Kivork must have shared them with his new employer, according to the complaint.

The lawsuit said Joby learned from a forensic investigation that Kivork had sent dozens of Joby's files to a personal email account and changed security permissions for hundreds of others so he could access them after he left.

Joby requested an unspecified amount of monetary damages and a court order blocking Archer from misusing its trade secrets.

Archer settled separate trade-secret claims from Boeing's Wisk air-taxi subsidiary in 2023.

data.title: Fitch Affirms Grupo Mexico at 'BBB+'; Outlook Stable
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-New York-20 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings has affirmed Grupo Mexico, S.A.B. de C.V.'s Long-Term Foreign and Local Currency Issuer Default Ratings (IDRs) at 'BBB+'. The Rating Outlook remains Stable.

Grupo Mexico's ratings are underpinned by the strong business profiles of its mining and rail subsidiaries, alongside a track record of robust capital structure and solid liquidity.

Grupo Mexico's main subsidiaries are Southern Copper Corporation (SSC; BBB+/Stable), one of the world's largest copper producers and leading low-cost operator, and GMexico Transportes S.A.B. de C.V. (GMXT; AAA(mex)/Stable), Mexico's largest railroad company that is a subsidiary of Grupo Ferroviario Mexicano, S.A. de C.V. (GFM; BBB+/Stable).

Fitch expects Grupo Mexico to remain discipline with its capital allocation strategy in terms of M&A and shareholder returns, maintaining EBITDA Net Leverage ratio below 0.5x.

Key Rating Drivers

Low-Cost Major Copper Producer: Grupo Mexico, via its 88.9% stake in Southern Copper Corporation (SCC; BBB+/Stable), is the fifth-largest global copper producer, with low costs and longest-life assets in investment-grade countries. SCC anchors the credit, accounting for about 72% of consolidated debt and EBITDA. SCC's industry-leading reported copper reserves support sustained long-term competitiveness. Operating risk in Americas Mining Corporation, SCC's parent company, is mitigated by diversified mines in Mexico (BBB-/Stable), Peru (BBB/Stable), and the U.S. (AA+/Stable).

Robust Capital Structure: Grupo Mexico, a debt-free holding company, had around USD3.4 billion of cash and marketable securities as of Sept. 30, 2025. Fitch forecasts its consolidated net leverage to stay below 0.5x, with a consolidated net cash position and average EBITDA around USD8.5 billion annually. Fitch expects SCC's operating cash flow to fund copper expansion, maintain net leverage below 1x, and continue distributing excess cash as dividends.

Diversified Business Profile: Grupo Mexico benefits from diversified dividends from SCC and from GMXT, Mexico's leading railway, which owns 74% of Grupo Ferroviario Mexicano, S.A. de C.V. (GFM; BBB+/Stable). It also operates subsidiaries in toll roads, oil rig leasing, construction, energy, infrastructure, and real estate under Mexico Proyectos y Desarrollos S.A. de C.V. In 2024, transportation and infrastructure contributed approximately 20% and 5% of Grupo Mexico's EBITDA, respectively.

Leading Mexican Railroad: GMXT's extensive rail network drives leading market share, strong pricing, and high-entry barriers in Mexico's duopolistic rail market. Rail competes with trucking but is favored for heavy, long-haul loads. Diversified customers and industries enhance cycle resilience, with access to the U.S., Canada, and ports. No industry exceeds 30% of sales, no customer over 5% of revenue (LTM 3Q25). Volumes should recover, supported by GMXT investments, long-term relationships, and industry rebounds.

Positive FCF: Grupo Mexico has achieved positive FCF generation for the sixth consecutive year, spurred by favorable copper prices. Copper fundamentals should remain strong, supported by the metal's central role in the energy transition and persistent industry supply constraints. Fitch projects FCF before dividends to remain at nearly USD3.0 billion in 2025 and 2026, despite increased investments in SCC's Tia Maria project. This would allow dividends to remain above USD2.0 billion in 2026.

Peer Analysis

Fitch benchmarks Grupo Mexico against global miners Anglo American Plc (BBB+/Negative), BHP Group Limited (A/Stable), and Rio Tinto Plc (A/Stable). Post-restructuring, Anglo American is similar in scale; its profitability is expected to be in the mid-40% range, below Grupo Mexico's roughly 50%.

Anglo's copper mine life of 41 years compares with SCC's 52. Anglo should remain more diversified, with about 60% operating cash flow from copper and 40% from iron ore. Grupo Mexico's mining division is projected to exceed 75% of EBITDA, driven by SCC's copper pipeline, while transportation stabilizes cash flows. Anglo's EBITDA net leverage is forecast at 1.0x-1.2x (2025-2026), versus Grupo Mexico below 0.5x.

Compared with 'A' peers, BHP and Rio Tinto have greater scale and diversification, while Grupo Mexico differentiates through cost leadership and superior copper reserves (SCC: 52 years vs. BHP: 16, Rio Tinto: 31). All maintain conservative capital structures, net leverage near or below 0.5x, and strong liquidity with balanced maturities.

Key Assumptions

-- Copper prices of USD9,500/MT in 2025, and Fitch's midcycle price assumptions for copper of USD9,000/MT in 2026 and USD8,500/MT in 2027;

-- SCC production volume at 959,000 tonnes in 2025, 910,000 in 2026, and 910,000 in 2027;

-- GMXT average revenue increase in U.S. dollar terms of 6% in 2025-2027;

-- GMXT average EBITDA margin calculated by Fitch of around 39% in 2025-2027;

-- Consolidated capex of USD2.2 billion in 2025, USD2.4 billion in 2026 and USD2.0 billion in 2027;

-- Consolidated dividends to controlling shareholders of USD2.3 billion in 2025 and average of USD2.5 billion annually during 2026-2027.

RATING SENSITIVITIES

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade:

-- Sustained consolidated net debt to EBITDA sharply above 2.0x;

-- Downgrade of SCC's IDR to 'bbb';

-- Net debt/EBITDA ratio sustained at SCC above 2x and prolonged deterioration of SCC's low-cost production status or copper fundamentals.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade:

-- An upgrade is unlikely in the short to medium term, given SCC' limited product and geographic diversification;

-- Upgrade of SCC's IDR to 'a-';

-- Net debt/EBITDA ratio sustained at SCC below 1x and a successful execution of greenfield or brownfield copper projects supported by a more favorable policy and operating environment for such projects in Peru;

-- Consolidated net debt/EBITDA sustained below 1x.

Liquidity and Debt Structure

Grupo Mexico exhibits strong liquidity with consolidated readily available cash and marketable securities of USD9.6 billion compared with total debt of USD9.4 billion as of Sept. 30, 2025.

Grupo Mexico has no holding company debt. Its subsidiaries have USD242 million of debt that amortizes in 2025, USD83 million maturing in 2026, and USD891 million in 2027, mostly from the transportation division. Around 72% of the company's consolidated debt is at SCC and approximately 70% of the consolidated debt amortizes after 2030.

Approximately 78% of the group's debt is U.S. dollar denominated with 91% of debt at a fixed rate. The company does not have a dividend payout policy, providing a flexible approach to liquidity during volatile periods and in times of large investments. The group's subsidiaries demonstrated strong access to international and local financing and are expected to maintain a proactive approach to liability management.

Issuer Profile

Grupo Mexico is the largest mining corporation in Mexico and is the fifth-largest copper producer in the world with integrated operations in Mexico, Peru and the US. It owns the largest multimodal rail service in Mexico and an Infrastructure Development Division.

REFERENCES FOR SUBSTANTIALLY MATERIAL SOURCE CITED AS KEY DRIVER OF RATING The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria. MACROECONOMIC ASSUMPTIONS AND SECTOR FORECASTS Click here to access Fitch's latest quarterly Global Corporates Sector Forecasts Monitor data file which aggregates key data points used in our credit analysis. Fitch's macroeconomic forecasts, commodity price assumptions, default rate forecasts, sector key performance indicators and sector-level forecasts are among the data items included. ESG Considerations

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit-neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores are not inputs in the rating process; they are an observation on the relevance and materiality of ESG factors in the rating decision. For more information on Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores, visit https://www.fitchratings.com/topics/esg/products#esg-relevance-scores.

data.title: A2 Milk Price Target Raised 9.2% to A$9.50/Share by Macquarie
Texas sues Bristol Myers, Sanofi for concealing blood thinner Plavix's alleged ineffectiveness
data.description:
Key points:
  • Defendants allegedly hid Plavix's ineffectiveness for some patients
  • Bristol Myers, Sanofi settled similar Hawaii claims for $700 million
  • Companies say Plavix is safe and effective
  • Paxton has sued other drugmakers, is running for US Senate

By Jonathan Stempel

Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton sued Bristol Myers Squibb and Sanofi on Thursday, accusing the drugmakers of failing to disclose that Plavix, which is used to prevent blood clots, does not work effectively for many patients.

In a complaint filed in a state court in Harrison County, Texas, Paxton said the drugmakers knew or should have known since 1998 that Plavix had "diminished effect" on Black, Asian and Pacific Islander patients, but no warning was added to the blood thinner's label until 2010.

Paxton said the lack of disclosure caused patients to be prescribed "substantially inadequate or inappropriate" medication that left them at greater risk of heart attacks or strokes.

The lawsuit seeks civil fines and other damages for alleged violations of the Texas Health Care Program Fraud Prevention Act.

"At bottom, defendants prioritized profits even if that meant putting patients' lives at risk," Paxton said in the complaint.

In a joint statement, Bristol Myers and Sanofi said they will vigorously defend against Paxton's lawsuit.

"The overwhelming body of scientific evidence demonstrates that Plavix is a safe and effective therapy, regardless of a patient’s race or genetics," the companies said.

The companies reached a $700 million settlement of similar claims by Hawaii in May. Bristol Myers is based in Princeton, New Jersey, and Sanofi is based in Paris.

Plavix's chemical name is clopidogrel.

Paxton has often turned to the courts to battle the pharmaceutical industry.

Last month he sued Tylenol maker Kenvue in a state court in Panola County, adjacent to Harrison County and bordering Louisiana, though a judge rejected his request for greater disclosures about the painkiller as well as a dividend freeze.

On Wednesday, Paxton announced a $41.5 million settlement with Pfizer over alleged quality control lapses in Quillivant XR, which treats attention deficit hyperactivity disorder in children.

Paxton is running for the U.S. Senate in 2026 and is expected to face incumbent Senator John Cornyn in the Republican primary.

data.title: Plank Ventures Ltd. Announces Completion of Sale of its Investment in Karve IT Ltd.
data.description:
(TheNewswire)


    Vancouver, B.C. – November 20, 2025 – TheNewswire - Plank Ventures Ltd. (“Plank” or
    the “Company”) (CSE:PLNK)
    announces that it has completed, pursuant to the
    binding share purchase agreement with Mobio Technologies Inc. (TSXV:
    MBO) (“Mobio”) previously announced on September 25, 2025, the
    sale of its investment in Karve IT Ltd. (“Karve”) consisting of
    310,000 common shares of
    Karve in exchange for 2,861,538 common shares of
    Mobio (the “Agreement”).


    Karve is a software development company operating under
    SaaS business model. Karve specializes in developing customer
    relationship management, franchise management and field services
    software which enables operational scalability and data driven
    workflows. Karve’s flagship product Move Right is in use across 20
    locations in North America.


    Transaction Details:


    Closing Date:
    The transaction has now closed following receipt of all required
    regulatory acceptances.


    Share Exchange: Plank sold its investment in Karve consisting of 310,000
    common shares to Mobio, in exchange for the issuance of 2,861,538
    Mobio shares from treasury. Plank’s shares in Karve were valued at
    $186,000, or $0.60 per share, and the number of Mobio shares issued
    was determined at the market price closest to the date of the purchase
    agreement.


    Finder’s Fees: No finder’s fees were paid in connection with the sale of
    Karve shares to Mobio.


    Non-Arm’s Length
    Transaction: The transaction is a related party
    transaction as Mobio and Plank have common control and management.
     The transaction is exempt from the formal
    valuation and shareholder approval requirements pursuant to sections
    5.5 and 5.7 respectively of Multilateral Instrument 61-101 Protection
    of Minority Security Holders in Special Transactions because the fair
    market value of the transaction does not exceed 25% of market
    capitalization of Plank.


    Following completion of the transaction, Plank no
    longer holds equity shares in Karve and now holds 2,861,538 shares of Mobio.


    About Plank Ventures Ltd.


    Plank is an investment company targeting investments
    and business opportunities in the technology arena, focusing on
    early-stage start-up companies that have an established customer and
    revenue base and are seeking funding for expansion.


    ON BEHALF OF THE BOARD OF
    DIRECTORS


    “Laurie Baggio”


    CEO and Director


    For additional information please contact:


    Laurie Baggio, CEO Tel:  778 300-7565


    Forward Looking Statements


    Other than statements of historical
    fact, all statements included in this news release, including, without
    limitation, statements regarding the sale of Karve shares to Mobio,
    future plans and objectives of Plank are forward-looking statements
    that involve various risks and uncertainties. There can be no
    assurance that such statements will prove to be accurate, and actual
    results and future events could differ materially from those anticipated in such
    statements. Factors that could cause actual results to differ
    materially from those expected by Plank are those risks described
    herein and from time to time, in the filings made by Plank with
    Canadian securities regulators. Those filings can be found on the
    Internet at: http://www.sedarplus.ca under the profile of Plank.
    Investors must not rely on the forward-looking statements.

    Copyright (c) 2025 TheNewswire - All rights reserved.

data.title: White House defends Trump calling a female reporter 'piggy'
data.description:
By Nandita Bose

The White House on Thursday defended President Donald Trump after he called a female reporter "piggy" as she questioned him about the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, saying the president's remarks reflected his frankness and transparency.

In the exchange aboard Air Force One last week, which has since gone viral, Trump leaned toward the reporter, pointed his finger and said, "Quiet, piggy" as she pressed him about a recently released Epstein email in which the New York financier claimed Trump "knew about the girls."

Asked on Thursday about the incident, White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said American voters re-elected Trump for his frankness and that reporters should appreciate his openness in answering questions.

"He calls out fake news when he sees it and gets frustrated with reporters who spread false information," Leavitt said during a White House briefing, offering no evidence of false information being reported. "But he also provides unprecedented access to the press and answers questions on a near-daily basis."

On Tuesday in the Oval Office, Trump called another female reporter "a terrible person" after she asked visiting Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman about the killing of journalist Jamal Khashoggi and asked Trump why he had not released the Epstein files.

Trump signed legislation on Wednesday ordering the Justice Department to release documents from its long-running investigation into Epstein after initially resisting having the files become public.

The Society of Professional Journalists issued a statement this week condemning Trump's disparaging language toward the reporters, noting his history of using demeaning language to discredit women. The White House declined to comment beyond Leavitt's earlier statement regarding Trump's "piggy" remark.

"Nobody expects presidents to be reporters' biggest fans," SPJ Executive Director Caroline Hendrie said. "But targeting women reporters with humiliating insults should not be tolerated."

data.title: Intouch Insight Q3 revenue stable, gross margin up
data.description:
Overview

  • Intouch Q3 revenue stable at C$6.63 mln, gross margin slightly up

  • Company completed acquisition of ClearPoint Solutions US

  • Operating expenses rose 11% as company invests in growth

Outlook

  • Intouch targets C$50 mln annual revenue by end of 2028

  • Company anticipates temporary profitability impact through 2026 and 2027

  • Intouch expects operating leverage and robust profitability approaching 2028

Result Drivers

  • SaaS REVENUE GROWTH - Co reports 3% organic growth in SaaS revenues, contributing to improved revenue mix

  • ACQUISITION - Co completed acquisition of ClearPoint Solutions, expanding market presence

  • INVESTMENT IMPACT - Operating expenses rose 11% as co invests in future growth, impacting profitability
